rag_rules: Place AGREE assumptions and guarantees in component type scopes when they refer to component ports.
Use AGREE syntax for assume, guarantee, eq, and const declarations within annex agree blocks.
Generate deterministic dependency guarantees that relate visible outputs to visible inputs.
Ensure type declarations in AGREE match the actual AADL data types of features.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must be placed in valid AADL scopes: component types for assumptions/guarantees, implementations for assignments.
Use initialized temporal recurrence for stateful equations and nested if-then-else for branching logic.
Do not reference implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes.
Validate that all identifiers used in AGREE expressions are visible in the current component scope.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};`.
Assumptions use `assume "label": expression;` and guarantees use `guarantee "label": expression;`.
Equations are declared with `eq name : type = expression;` and constants with `const name : type = value;`.
Logical operators in AGREE include `and`, `or`, `not(...)`, and implication `=>`.
anti_patterns: Avoid placing implementation-level bindings in component type annexes.
Do not reference AADL properties like Deadline or Period as AGREE variables.
Do not invent new identifiers; only use visible features from the AADL model.
Do not generate guarantees that depend on subcomponent ports not visible at the type level.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations and timing constraints
Model fusion and architecture grounding details
Specific AADL package or data type definitions
